prompt: Using RFdiffusion, design a de novo repeat protein (150–200 residues) that disrupts HIV-1 capsid protein (CA) hexamer assembly by occupying the central pore of the hexamer interface, as an antiretroviral strategy. The designed protein should achieve sub-micromolar affinity for CA hexamers. outline a methodology for: (1) RFdiffusion backbone generation targeting the CA hexamer pore (PDB 3H47), (2) ProteinMPNN sequence design with symmetry-matching constraints, (3) assembly disruption modelling using molecular dynamics. Discuss delivery considerations for intracellular targeting of capsid during reverse transcription.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05); target: Capsid protein CA (p24); organism: Human immunodeficiency virus 1; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: core assembly protein (HIV-1 capsid hexamer structural subunit)
